title: tephrocactus neuquensis
intro: Tephrocactus neuquensis, also known as the Neuquén ball cactus, is a species of cactus in the genus Tephrocactus, characterized by its globose stems and sharp, hooked spines. The plant is used in traditional medicine in some parts of South America, where it is valued for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Tephrocactus neuquensis are believed to include the treatment of various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Tephrocactus neuquensis is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is not widely recognized or recommended in modern medicine.
